title: Di'kut

{{Quote|Is it okay to scream hysterically now, Sarge?|Di'kut|<ref name="Orders" />}}
'''Di'kut''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who served in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] as a [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Outfitted with [[Color/Legends|green]]-marked [[Katarn-class commando armor/Legends|''Katarn''-class]] [[Commando/Legends|commando]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]], and in possession of a sarcastic sense of humor, Di'kut served with [[Aiwha Squad]] during the later portion of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], along with fellow commandos [[Zag]], [[Tyto]], and [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Sarge (clone commando)|Sarge]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Di'kut and his [[Aiwha/Legends|Aiwha]] [[Squad/Legends|squadmates]] were involved in the [[Battle of Garqi (Clone Wars)|Battle]] of [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]], where they were tasked with searching for civilian refugees following a Republic [[Bomb/Legends|bombing]] campaign. Discovering a [[Youngling/Legends|child]] by the name of [[Evan]], the four clones escorted the boy to a nearby refugee camp overseen by [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Traavis]]. There, the squad received the command to carry out [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] from [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and Di'kut opened fire on his former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in unison with his clone brothers, [[Death/Legends|killing]] Traavis.

==Biography==
{{Quote|This ain't Kashyyyk. Take it easy, Zag.|Di'kut, to [[Zag]] during their mission to Garqi|<ref name="Orders" />}}
Di'kut was the nickname of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. Born on the [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]],<ref name="DB Clone troopers" /> located in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] region,<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Di'kut was created to serve as a [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]].<ref name="Orders" /> At some point in his life, he adopted or was labeled with the name "Di'kut", a Mandalorian word which amounted to "idiot" in ''Mando'a''.<ref name="Mando'a">{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/html/mando.htm|text=Ke jorhaa'ir Mando'a!|author=Karen Traviss|archivedate=20090923140534}}m</ref> When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|broke out]] between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Di'kut departed Kamino and was brought into active service within the Grand [[Army/Legends|Army's]] [[Special Operations Brigade/Legends|Special Operations Brigade]], under the command of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Arligan Zey]].<ref name="Republic Commando: Triple Zero">''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''</ref>

[[File:Squad.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Di'kut and his squadmates engage a homing spider droid]]
By the [[19 BBY/Legends|third]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the Clone Wars, Di'kut was serving as a member of [[Aiwha Squad]], a [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]] [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of clone [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] that also included the commandos [[Zag]] and [[Tyto]], and lead by [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Sarge (clone commando)|Sarge]]. In the final [[Galactic Standard Calendar|days]] of the war, Di'kut and his clone brothers were sent to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]], [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]], where, following a Republic [[Bomb/Legends|bombing]] campaign against the Separatist presence, [[Aiwha/Legends|Aiwha]] Squad was tasked with searching for survivors. Upon investigating a badly damaged [[Farm/Legends|farmhouse]], Di'kut and the others came across a [[Youngling/Legends|young child]] by the name of [[Evan]]. With his [[Parent/Legends|parents]] [[Death/Legends|dead]], the responsibility of seeing the boy to the nearby [[Garqi refugee camp|refugee camp]] fell to the commandos; Evan, however, was reluctant to go with the clones and attempted to flee their custody and return to his ruined home, unintentially coming upon a [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droid]]. Di'kut and the other commandos attacked the [[spider droid]], destroying it and saving Evan, earning the young boy's admiration. While Di'kut had unhesitantly rushed to defend the boy, once Evan was safe, he sarcastically remarked that he would let the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] kill him if he tried to run away again.<ref name="Orders" />

That night, the clones and their young charge made camp in the Garqian wilderness, and Di'kut sat with his brothers around a fire while Sarge explained the life of a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] to Evan. The following morning, Aiwha Squad arrived at the refugee camp under the oversight of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Traavis]]. Di'kut and his squadmates were congratulated by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] for their efforts, and he informed them of the death of General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]—the [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]—at the [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of [[High Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. The meeting would be soured, however, by the issuing of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]],<ref name="Orders" /> one of the one hundred and fifty [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|contingency orders]] of the Grand Army and which detailed that the Jedi had been declared traitors to the Republic marked for execution on sight. Having come directly from [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="Republic Commando: True Colors">''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref> Sarge ordered the squad to open fire on General Traavis; Di'kut and his squadmates complied, [[Blaster/Legends|shooting]] and killing the Jedi General in accordance with the Chancellor's order.<ref name="Orders" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Try that again, kid, and I'll let the droids fry you...fierfek...stupid little son of a nerf herder...|Di'kut, upon saving [[Evan]] from a spider droid after the boy tried to run away|<ref name="Orders" />}}
Di'kut possessed a sarcastic sense of humor. At times, it manifested as irreverent joviality, but could come off as biting cynicism when frustrated.<ref name="Orders" /> His off-beat humor was likely the source of the nickname he inherited: ''di'kut'' was a Mandalorian curse approximate to "moron", and literally translating as "someone who forgets to put their pants on" in [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']].<ref name="Mando'a" /><ref name="Encyclopedia" /> Di'kut favored the anti-[[Armor/Legends|armor]] [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]] attachment of his [[DC-17m Interchangeable Weapon System/Legends|DC-17 blaster rifle]] in combat, and kept additional [[Grenade/Legends|ordnance]] for the weapon on his [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]]. He also held a preference for the [[Color/Legends|color]] green, and decorated his [[Katarn-class commando armor/Legends|''Katarn''-class commando armor]] with custom green markings.<ref name="Orders" />

==Behind the scenes==
Di'kut made his first appearance in the [[2005]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story, [[Orders (comic story)|''Orders'']], from the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 4|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume'' 4]] of the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|''Clone Wars Adventures'']] comic series. The story was [[Author/Legends|authored]] by [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], and Di'kut was drawn by the [[Fillbach Brothers|Fillbach brothers]], [[Matthew Fillbach|Matthew]] and [[Shawn Fillbach|Shawn]]. He was later given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[2008]].

==Appearances==
